plan.original_query: What controls apply to API security?

steps.search_query: API access control and authentication for application programming interfaces
steps.purpose: Find controls requiring authentication, authorization, session management, and access enforcement specifically for APIs and programmatic interfaces.

steps.search_query: Secure API communications and protection of data in transit for APIs
steps.purpose: Find controls addressing confidentiality and integrity of API traffic, encryption, transport protections, and message-level protections for APIs.

steps.search_query: API input validation, application-layer filtering, and protection against injection and abuse
steps.purpose: Find controls that require input validation, boundary protection, rate limiting, monitoring, and protections against common API attacks (injection, parameter tampering, denial of service).

documents.title: Service Identification and Authentication
documents.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate [Assignment: system services and applications] before establishing communications with devices, users, or other services or applications.

documents.title: Assert and Enforce Application Access
Access Only
documents.description: Provide access from a single device to computing platforms, applications, or data residing in multiple different security domains, while preventing information flow between the different security domains.

documents.title: Domain Authentication
documents.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate source and destination points by [Selection (one or more): organization, system, application, service, individual] for information transfer.

documents.title: Privileged Commands and Access
documents.description:
(a) Authorize the execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access only in a format that provides assessable evidence and for the following needs: [Assignment: organization-defined needs] ; and
(b) Document the rationale for remote access in the security plan for the system.

step.search_query: Secure API communications and protection of data in transit for APIs
step.purpose: Find controls addressing confidentiality and integrity of API traffic, encryption, transport protections, and message-level protections for APIs.

documents.title: Cryptographic Protection
documents.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to [Selection (one or more): prevent unauthorized disclosure of information; detect changes to information] during transmission.

documents.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
documents.description: Protect the [Selection (one or more): confidentiality; integrity] of transmitted information.

documents.title: Cryptographic Binding
documents.description: Implement [Assignment: mechanisms or techniques] to bind security and privacy attributes to transmitted information.

documents.title: Pre- and Post-transmission Handling
documents.description: Maintain the [Selection (one or more): confidentiality; integrity] of information during preparation for transmission and during reception.

documents.title: Secure Name/Address Resolution Service (Recursive or Caching Resolver)
documents.description: Request and perform data origin authentication and data integrity verification on the name/address resolution responses the system receives from authoritative sources.

step.search_query: API input validation, application-layer filtering, and protection against injection and abuse

documents.title: Layered Structures
documents.description: Implement security functions as a layered structure minimizing interactions between layers of the design and avoiding any dependence by lower layers on the functionality or correctness of higher layers.

documents.title: Injection Prevention
documents.description: Prevent untrusted data injections.

documents.title: Review and Resolve Errors
documents.description: Review and resolve input validation errors within [Assignment: organization-defined time period].

response.answer:
Applicable controls for API security from the supplied list are:

- Require strong identification/authentication of services and apps that communicate via APIs: uniquely identify and authenticate system services and applications before establishing communications [ia-9].

- Protect confidentiality and integrity of API traffic in transit: implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure and to detect changes during transmission [sc-8.1], and more generally protect confidentiality and/or integrity of transmitted information [sc-8]. Also maintain confidentiality and integrity during preparation for transmission and reception (pre/post handling) [sc-8.2]. Where needed, bind security or privacy attributes to the transmitted API data (cryptographic binding) [sc-16.3].

- Ensure name/address resolution used by APIs is trustworthy: request and perform data origin authentication and integrity verification on name/address resolution responses (secure resolver) to avoid DNS-based attacks on API endpoints [sc-21].

- Enforce application-level access controls for API clients/consumers: require applications to assert needed access at installation, provide enforcement to prevent unauthorized access, and approve access changes after installation [ac-3.12]. Also support domain-level authentication of source and destination points for information transfer (identify/authenticate organization/system/application/service/individual) [ac-4.17].

- Limit cross-domain information flow for multi-domain access scenarios: provide access from a single device to resources in multiple security domains while preventing information flow between domains (useful for APIs bridging domains) [ac-4.22].

- Control privileged remote API actions: authorize privileged commands and access via remote access only in formats that provide assessable evidence, and document remote access rationale in the system security plan [ac-17.4].

- Architect APIs with layered security: implement security functions in layered structures minimizing inter-layer interactions and avoiding lower-layer dependence on higher-layer correctness [sc-3.5].

- Validate and sanitize inputs to APIs to prevent injections and resolve input validation errors promptly: prevent untrusted data injections [si-10.6] and review/resolve input validation errors within an organization-defined time period [si-10.2].

If you need mapping of these controls to specific API safeguards (e.g., TLS for transport, mTLS for service auth, input validation libraries, API gateway enforcement), say so and I will map each control to those concrete measures.

plan.original_query: What policies relate to logging and monitoring?

steps.search_query: logging and monitoring of information system activities and audit records
steps.purpose: Find NIST SP 800-53 controls that require creation, management, and review of audit logs and continuous monitoring of system activity (audit generation, log retention, protection, review, and monitoring processes).

step.search_query: logging and monitoring of information system activities and audit records
step.purpose: Find NIST SP 800-53 controls that require creation, management, and review of audit logs and continuous monitoring of system activity (audit generation, log retention, protection, review, and monitoring processes).

documents.title: Event Logging
Integrated Analysis of Audit Records
documents.description: Integrate analysis of audit records with analysis of [Selection (one or more): vulnerability scanning information; performance data; system monitoring information; [Assignment: data/information collected from other sources] ] to further enhance the ability to identify inappropriate or unusual activity.

documents.title: Audit Record Generation
Logging and Review
documents.description:
(a) Log [Assignment: organization-defined audit events] for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions; and
(b) Review the audit records of the maintenance and diagnostic sessions to detect anomalous behavior.

documents.title: Central Review and Analysis
documents.description: Provide and implement the capability to centrally review and analyze audit records from multiple components within the system.

documents.title: Event Logging
Integrated Analysis of Audit Records
documents.description: Integrate analysis of audit records with analysis of [Selection (one or more): vulnerability scanning information; performance data; system monitoring information; [Assignment: data/information collected from other sources] ] to further enhance the ability to identify inappropriate or unusual activity.

documents.title: Audit Record Generation
Logging and Review
documents.description:
(a) Log [Assignment: organization-defined audit events] for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions; and
(b) Review the audit records of the maintenance and diagnostic sessions to detect anomalous behavior.

documents.title: Central Review and Analysis
documents.description: Provide and implement the capability to centrally review and analyze audit records from multiple components within the system.

response.answer:
The supplied controls that relate to logging and monitoring are the following:

- AU‑2 (Event Logging) — defines what event types the system must be capable of logging, coordination with other organizational entities on logging needs, specifying which event types to log and their frequency or triggering situations, providing a rationale for adequacy for investigations, and periodic review/update of the selected event types [au-2].

- AU‑12 (Audit Record Generation) — requires audit record generation capability for the event types identified under AU‑2a for specified system components, allows designated personnel/roles to select which event types are logged by specific components, and requires generated audit records to include the contents defined in AU‑3 for the event types in AU‑2c [au-12].

- AU‑6.4 (Central Review and Analysis) — requires capability to centrally review and analyze audit records from multiple system components, supporting consolidated monitoring and analysis [au-6.4].

- AU‑6.5 (Integrated Analysis of Audit Records) — requires integration of audit-record analysis with other data sources (e.g., vulnerability scanning, performance data, system monitoring, or other organization-defined sources) to improve detection of inappropriate or unusual activity [au-6.5].

- MA‑4.1 (Logging and Review — Maintenance) — requires logging of organization-defined audit events for nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic sessions and review of those maintenance/diagnostic audit records to detect anomalous behavior [ma-4.1].

If you need policy text or template language for any one of these controls (for example, an organizational policy statement implementing AU‑2 or AU‑6.4), indicate which control(s) and I will draft it using only the corresponding control content above.

plan.original_query: What is the capital of France?

steps.search_query: Geographic knowledge: capital city of France
steps.purpose: Find a control-style statement that identifies or references the capital city of France (Paris) as the primary required fact.

step.search_query: Geographic knowledge: capital city of France
